title: Commentary on a method for testing resistance to shocks
author: Narayan
description: This note tours the Narayan (2020a: Has COVID-19 Changed Exchange Rate Resistance to Shocks?) approach to testing for resistance of a time-series variable to shocks. We take a step-by-step account of this approach and demonstrate its applicability with respect to the crude oil price.•The approach entails steps (1) to (8), as outline in the paper.•Future researchers will find this method useful in evaluating the resistance of variables to not only COVID-19 shocks but to any shock which has had a sufficiently long life.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: The Narayan [26] resistance methodology is in turn based on the Narayan and Popp [23], see also Narayan and Popp [22], unit root (NP-UR) test.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 1: Implement the NP-UR model that allows for two endogenous breaks in the level. There are no breaks in the trend.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 2: Choose an appropriate trimming factor to account for tails of the data. Typically, a 10%-20% trimming factor is employed depending on the sample size.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 3: Choose the initial data sample window to generate time-varying β. Narayan (2020) recommend a window size of 20%. Alternative window sizes may be motivated based on sample size.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 4: Run the following regression model using ordinary least squares:(1)OILt=α+βOILt−1+λt+κ1BRB,1′+κ2BRB,2′+δ1DU1,t−1′+δ2DU2,t−1′+∑j=1kπjΔOILt−j+et

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: The variable which we consider for the test of resistance is the WTI crude oil price (OIL).

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 5: Choose the lag length, k, which should be set high enough to accommodate serial correlation. When done, use one of the lag length selection criteria to select the optimal lag length.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 6: Obtain the BRB,1and BRB,2 which denote, respectively, the first and second break dates; and DU1 and DU2 are the level break dummy variables.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: 0Step 7: Test the null hypothesis of a unit root by setting β=1 against the alternative hypothesis that β<1.

steps_raw.title: Step-by-Step method/approach
steps_raw.instruction: Step 8: Use the critical values from NP [23] to decide on the rejection or otherwise of the unit root null hypothesis.
